title: Microgramma galatheae
taxonomy: Microgramma galatheae is classified within the plant family Marattiaceae, which includes a group of ancient ferns. This species belongs to the genus Microgramma, a group of epiphytic ferns. The classification of Microgramma galatheae is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, and class Polypodiopsida. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This species is known for its unique, delicate fronds and is often used as an ornamental plant in tropical gardens.

intro: Microgramma galatheae, commonly known as Galatheaea or Rattlesnake fern, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant is known for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Microgramma galatheae include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory ailments.
